C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: **Core text:** Exodus 12 · **NT fulfillment:** 1 Corinthians 5:7 · John 19:33–36 · 1 Peter 1:18–19 **Thread (one spine, hook→landing):** The Passover instructions are strangely over-specified — a lamb without blemish, inspected for days, killed by the whole assembly, its blood marking the doorframe, *not one bone broken*.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: It is whether you will stop trusting your own door, and rest under the blood of the Lamb who was slain for you.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: Ten centuries before the cross, a song recorded exactly how a dying man's clothes would be divided up.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: They rolled dice for the clothes off His back, never seeing what He was really doing — laying down His life to win you back.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: At the cross they shook their heads and sneered the very taunt a psalm had recorded a thousand years before.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: He could have come down in an instant — He chose to stay, with you in view.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: The loneliest sentence Jesus ever spoke wasn't His own line — He was quoting.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: However far you've run, the way home is open — and He opened it from the dark.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: Most people stop reading Psalm twenty-two at the cross.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: He is calling you into that family.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: A psalm's last line and Jesus' last breath in John close on the very same note: it is done.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: Nothing — only Someone to come home to: the One who said it is done.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: Psalm twenty-two opens with one forsaken man dying alone — and ends with every nation on earth.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: The song that began with one man dying alone has swept the whole earth; the Lord it promised still has room for you to turn to Him.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: A king described a dying body so exactly it reads like an eyewitness account of a crucifixion he never saw.
C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.cta: He was crushed in your place.

C:\\Users\\sanjay\\PycharmProjects\\Jesu.hook: The One who made every ocean gasped two words on a cross: I thirst.
